description: Binary market on whether the fed funds rate upper bound stays above 3.50% after the December 2026 FOMC meeting. At 63%, the market implies a ~37% chance of 100+ bps in cuts from the current 4.25-4.50% range by year-end.
question: Will the federal funds rate upper bound be above 3.50% after the Fed's December 2026 meeting?

resolution_logic.summary: Resolves Yes if the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is greater than 3.50% following the December 9, 2026 FOMC meeting, as published on the Federal Reserve's official website.

triggers.description: Upper bound of federal funds rate target range exceeds 3.50%

resolution_logic.ambiguity_notes: Uses upper bound (not lower bound) — at current 4.25-4.50% range, rate would need to be cut by 100+ bps to resolve No. This implies at least four 25bp cuts by December. Market at 63% Yes means ~37% chance of 100+ bps in cuts by year-end. Polymarket's combo markets use lower bound — different reference point creates subtle pricing differences.

catalyst_dates.event: June FOMC decision + dot plot update
September FOMC decision + dot plot update
December FOMC decision + dot plot update

cross_platform_link.structural_notes: Polymarket's closest equivalent is the 'Warsh confirmed & rate ≤ 2.5%' combo market at 11%. But it bundles two independent events (Fed Chair nomination + rate level) and uses lower bound instead of upper bound. Not directly comparable — Kalshi isolates the rate question, Polymarket combines it with a political outcome. This structural divergence is itself a finding.

related_markets.notes: December hold at 67% — if the Fed holds through December, rate stays well above 3.50% (currently 4.25-4.50%)
April hold at 95% — near-term anchor suggesting no immediate cuts
